User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Eritas Gateway application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

signUpDate.description: The date and time when the user signed up.

lastLogin.description: The date and time when the user last logged in.

profilePicture.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

googleId.description: Google identifier for users who signed up using Google.

appleId.description: Apple identifier for users who signed up using Apple.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership is enforced, allowing each user to only access their own document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning: This Firestore data structure is designed for the Eritas Gateway bus booking application. It prioritizes security and scalability following the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). User data is stored in a dedicated collection, ensuring clear ownership and simplified security rules. This design avoids complex hierarchical authorization dependencies and promotes atomic operations (transactions/batches). Because this application only contains Users, the design is quite simple and future entities can be easily integrated into this basic architecture. Path-Based ownership is used here because that is the correct access model.
